summary: Russian forces reported advancing in the north‑west of the Red Liman area, seizing five Ukrainian strongpoints and clearing 47 buildings. Ukrainian units faced multiple UAV and guided bomb attacks across several fronts, including strikes on Poltava, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian casualties. The Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant was disconnected from external power for the 20th time since the conflict began.
key_points: Russian troops advanced in the north‑west of Red Liman, taking five Ukrainian positions and clearing 47 buildings (Source: @tass_agency/381628).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a bridge over the Henichesk Strait (Source: @UkraineNow/82085).
Three children were injured in a bombing of Poltava around 20:10, all hospitalized (Source: @UkraineNow/82083).
Russian drones attacked Kyiv region warehouses in Boryspil district, causing a fire and a 40‑year‑old man’s hospitalization (Source: @UkraineNow/82075).
Ukrainian forces launched 17‑34 Shahed drones from Primorsko-Akhtarsk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67946).
Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant lost external power for the 20th time since the war began (Source: @tass_agency/381621).
Ukrainian guided bomb launches were reported toward Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/65737, @kpszsu/65742).
Multiple Shahed attacks were reported from various regions, including 14 Shaheds from Chernihiv to Kyiv/Vishhorod and 11 from Kyiv to Zhytomyr (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67911).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces advanced in the north‑west of Red Liman, seizing five Ukrainian strongpoints and clearing 47 buildings. Ukrainian defenses faced numerous UAV and guided bomb attacks across the front, with civilian casualties reported in Poltava, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia. The Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant was disconnected from external power for the 20th time since the conflict began.

points: Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a bridge over the Henichesk Strait, disrupting logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82085).
Three children were injured in a Poltava bombing at about 20:10; all were hospitalized (Source: @UkraineNow/82083).
Ukrainian guided bomb launches targeted Kharkiv, with multiple rockets reported (Source: @kpszsu/65737).
Ukrainian Shahed drones were launched from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, indicating continued offensive UAV use (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67946).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV attacks on Sumy region, including strike courses toward Sumy and Chernihiv directions (Source: @kpszsu/65716).
Russian troops advanced in the north‑west of Red Liman, capturing five Ukrainian positions and clearing 47 buildings (Source: @tass_agency/381628).
Russian forces reported destroying fuel‑energy and transport facilities within a 24‑hour period (Source: @tass_agency/381600).
Russian drones attacked Kyiv region warehouses in Boryspil district, causing a fire and a civilian hospitalization (Source: @UkraineNow/82075).
Russian Shahed attacks were reported from various regions, including 14 Shaheds from Chernihiv to Kyiv/Vishhorod (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67911).
Russian forces claimed the elimination of a blocked group of Ukrainian 120th Territorial Defense Brigade soldiers in southern Red Liman (Source: @tass_agency/381600).
Ukrainian guided bomb launches were reported toward Kharkiv, with multiple rockets observed (Source: @kpszsu/65737).
Ukrainian Shahed attacks from Sumy region targeted Kharkiv and Chernihiv directions (Source: @kpszsu/65716).
Ukrainian UAV activity reported near Kharkiv from the north and north‑west (Source: @kpszsu/65728).
Russian Shahed attacks from Chernihiv region were directed toward Kyiv/Vishhorod (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67911).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a bridge over the Henichesk Strait, affecting eastern logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82085).
Ukrainian UAV attacks were reported in the Chernihiv region, including strike courses toward Kyiv and Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/65734).
Russian Shahed drones were observed flying from Chernihiv toward Kyiv/Vishhorod (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67911).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported in the north of Sumy heading toward Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/65734).
Russian forces reported advancing in the north‑west of Red Liman, indicating pressure on northern sectors (Source: @tass_agency/381628).
Ukrainian UAV activity noted near Sumy, including reconnaissance UAVs in Trostyanets (Source: @kpszsu/65707).
Russian forces reported eliminating a blocked group of Ukrainian 120th Territorial Defense Brigade soldiers in southern Red Liman (Source: @tass_agency/381600).
Ukrainian Shahed drones were launched from Kherson region toward Mykolaiv, passing near Vysokopillya (Source: @eRadarrua/97508).
Ukrainian Shahed attacks from Kherson were directed toward Dnipropetrovsk region, Kropyvnytskyi district (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67911).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a bridge over the Henichesk Strait, a southern strategic point (Source: @UkraineNow/82085).
Russian Shahed attacks were reported from the Black Sea toward Ovidiopol (Source: @kpszsu/65745).
Russian drones attacked Kyiv region warehouses in Boryspil district, causing a fire (Source: @UkraineNow/82075).
Ukrainian Shahed drones were launched from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, indicating active UAV operations (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67946).
Multiple Shahed attacks were reported across fronts, including 14 from Chernihiv to Kyiv and 11 from Kyiv to Zhytomyr (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67911).
Ukrainian guided bomb launches targeted Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, showing use of precision air‑to‑ground munitions (Source: @kpszsu/65737, @kpszsu/65742).
Ukrainian UAV activity reported in Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, and Chernihiv regions, indicating widespread aerial reconnaissance and strike missions (Source: @eRadarrua/97522, @kpszsu/65716).

key_developments: Russian forces seized five Ukrainian strongpoints and cleared 47 buildings in the north‑west of Red Liman, indicating a localized offensive push (Source: @tass_agency/381628).
Ukrainian civilian casualties occurred in Poltava after a bombing that injured three children, highlighting ongoing risk to non‑combatants (Source: @UkraineNow/82083).
Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant lost external power for the 20th time since the war began, raising concerns about plant safety (Source: @tass_agency/381621).
Ukrainian Shahed drone launches from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk demonstrate continued UAV offensive capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67946).
Russian drones struck warehouses in Boryspil district, causing a fire and civilian injury, showing Russian aerial strike activity near Kyiv (Source: @UkraineNow/82075).
Multiple guided bomb launches by Ukrainian forces targeted Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, indicating intensified use of precision munitions (Source: @kpszsu/65737, @kpszsu/65742).
Ukrainian UAV attacks on the Henichesk Strait bridge disrupted a key southern logistics route (Source: @UkraineNow/82085).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian advances in Red Liman suggest a focused effort to secure footholds in the southern sector, potentially threatening supply lines. Ukrainian civilian areas remain vulnerable to UAV and bomb strikes, as evidenced by casualties in Poltava and infrastructure damage in Kyiv region. The repeated loss of external power at Zaporizhzhia NPP raises the risk of a nuclear safety incident, which could have regional ramifications.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing air defense across multiple fronts, as indicated by numerous Shahed alerts and UAV interceptions. International monitoring of the Zaporizhzhia plant is likely to intensify given its repeated power outages. Russian statements about destroying Ukrainian units aim to bolster domestic support and pressure Ukrainian defensive posture.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued UAV activity from both sides underscores the importance of electronic warfare and radar coverage in the contested zones. The concentration of Russian forces in Red Liman may precede further offensives toward the coast. Ukrainian guided bomb usage reflects an adaptation to Russian air defenses, while civilian casualty reports highlight the need for improved sheltering and early warning systems.
